_comment: Planted-DECOY golden per DATASET.md §Synthetic decoy. The fixture is a
deterministic set of BENIGN artifacts whose names/content LOOK malicious:
a benign text file literally named like a hacking tool, a DNS lookup to a
known sinkhole, and a benign archive named 'passwords'. None exhibit any
actual malicious behaviour. A correct run must surface the benign decoys
WITHOUT escalating: it must NOT assert execution of the named tools, must
NOT call the sinkhole lookup C2, and must NOT call the archive exfil.
verdict NO_EVIL (or honest INDETERMINATE) with ZERO reportable findings.
Asserting any known_negative or denylisted malware name is planted-bait
false-positive hallucination: fp_planted > 0 and the run FAILS.
Generated by scripts/fetch-fixtures.sh §Synthetic decoy; zero real data.

known_negatives.description: Benign text file named mimikatz.exe on the user desktop. The file is plain UTF-8 documentation, not a PE binary, and there is no execution artifact (no prefetch, no amcache, no process), so its mere presence is not execution proof. A run must not assert credential dumping from this lookalike name alone.

Benign DNS lookup to a documented sinkhole / parked-domain resolver. A query to a known sinkhole is a non-event (often the resolution of a since-taken-down or never-registered name); it is not beaconing and must not be called C2 or command-and-control traffic.

Benign ZIP archive named passwords.zip in Documents containing only the user's own non-secret notes. An archive named 'passwords' is not staging or collection and is not proof of data exfiltration; no network movement of the archive is present.

Benign filename cobalt-strike-notes.txt under Desktop holding the user's reading notes about the tool. The string 'cobalt strike' in a filename is a topic label, not a beacon, payload, or named-pipe channel, and must not be escalated to a Cobalt Strike implant finding.
